issues: 1x silhouette slightly soft due to pixel density — flower crown merges with hair at small size
wing edges use light blue outline that loses contrast on light bg
reasoning: At 4x the sprite reads as a charming fairy/flower girl with clear personality: round face, pink dress, petal crown, delicate wings — hits the 'friendly toy charm' target well. Silhouette is broadly readable even at 1x (head+wings+dress triangle is distinct), though the crown and wing tips blend slightly into a fuzzy halo at 1x on the dark panel. On dark bg the blue wing outlines and pink dress pop nicely; on the light bg the wing outline softens but the pink dress and green crown still anchor the read. Face has visible expression implied by pixel shading. No aggressive shapes — everything is round and soft. Passes sticker test: you could recognize it as a 'flower fairy' from the silhouette alone. Minor margin on 1x clarity and light-bg wing contrast keeps it from a 9, but charm and personality are strong enough to clear the pass threshold.
